Empires & Puzzles Karnov 5-Star Legendary Dark Purple from League of Villains Family Review
He hits like a wet noodle in raids, but on Titans, he's a god. We break down why Karnov is the most confusingly designed Villain in the game and if he's worth your tabbards today.
Karnov Review: The Forgotten Titan King Who Lost His Shoes
Karnov: The Forgotten Titan King Who Lost His Shoes
classic who has been confusing players since 2021. He’s a

hero who hits everyone, messes with the puzzle board, and used to actively sabotage his own team (we’ll get to that later). In the modern era of 2026, the base version of Karnov is largely a relic, overshadowed by faster, harder-hitting heroes. However, he holds onto a very specific, very sticky niche: making yellow Titans cry.
version of Karnov. If you are looking for the version wearing pajamas and wielding a toothbrush, check out our review of his Costume.

Visually, Karnov is what happens when you mix a street brawler with a jewelry store heist. He’s shirtless, muscular, and adorned with a massive gold lion medallion that probably weighs more than a 3-star hero. His most defining feature? The complete lack of footwear. The man is fighting interdimensional wars barefoot. That’s either bravery or he spent all his gem budget on the necklace.
, he is statistically weaker than many modern 4-star heroes (some of whom break 800 power easily with costumes). His Attack stat of

was impressive in 2021 but is laughable compared to modern attackers pushing 1300+. His defense and health are similarly outdated. Without his costume bonus or Limit Breaks, he is essentially made of paper in today's meta.

This is his bread and butter. He alters all
This is huge. It turns your tile damage into a nuclear weapon.
If an enemy casts a special, the tiles weaken.

The damage component is negligible. You bring Karnov for the tile buff. That +64% attack on tiles stack with standard attack buffs (like Rigard or C.Rigard), making him indispensable for high scores against Holy Titans.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The League of Villains family bonus was once unique but is now just "nice to have."
bonus offers a chance to heal for a percentage of damage dealt.
It's a nice sustain mechanic, effectively giving him minor lifesteal. However, since his damage is low, the healing is often negligible unless he hits a defense-downed team.
The base version of Karnov tragically lacks the passive skills given to newer heroes or his costumed variant.
on a Slow, squishy hero is... okay. It might punish an AoE hitter who triggers it, but Karnov usually dies before he can make effective use of it.
When released, Karnov was controversial. He originally had a debuff that
slowed the mana generation of Holy allies
, making him impossible to run in rainbow or 3-2 teams with yellow heroes. He was hated by many for this "anti-synergy." That drawback was eventually removed, but the stigma remained.
He has been power-crept into oblivion. His stats are obsolete, his speed is too slow for his impact, and his damage is pitiful.
Do not use him. He will die before charging, and if he does charge, he will tickle the enemy.
He is still a top-tier Titan specialist for his tile buff. If you don't have a better board buffer (like a specific event hero or his costume), base Karnov is still on the team.

, adds extra damage vs Holy, and keeps the tile buff.

The stats bonus alone makes the costume mandatory if you want him to survive anything stronger than a stiff breeze. Even if you prefer the Monk class, you should level the costume just for the stat boost to the base form.
Since you are likely only using him for Titans, your path is simple.
Maximizes his tile damage stat, which is the only thing that matters against Titans.
You can use potions to keep him alive on Titans. You can't use potions to make him hit harder (beyond items).
. If you are using base Karnov in 2026, it's for tile damage. Period.
One of the few heroes who can buff specific tile colors.
Crucial for high scores on Yellow Titans.
Can resist mana control or defense down (occasionally).
Ancient stats mean he dies if a modern sniper even looks at him.
Useless in almost all modes except Titans and maybe Rush Attack (if you have literally no one else).
This is his home. He lives here.
usable as a tank or flank if you lack better purple options, but don't expect miracles.
Stacking Attack Up, Elemental Defense Down, Normal Defense Down, and Karnov's Tile Buff results in exponential damage.
Hansel, Gretel, Proteus. Karnov is slow; if you stop his mana, he is a dead weight.
If Karnov gets hit with Attack Down, his weak special hits for 0 damage.

If you pull Karnov early, he can be a serviceable "nuke" for map stages because he hits everyone. Do not prioritize him over versatile 4-stars like Rigard or Tiburtus. Level him only when you start caring about Titan scores.

You know what he is. He's a tile buffer. If you have his costume, wear it. If you don't, keep him at 3/70 or 4/80 solely for Titans. Do not Limit Break the base version unless you have money to burn; save the Aethers for the Costume or a modern hero.
"Is it just me or does this guy look like a street fighter?"
- A common sentiment regarding his visual design.
"Why does a slow hero have to include a disadvantage to holy allies?"
- The community was baffled by his original design flaw.
"He is useless on defence tbh... He’s only meant for a dark stack."
- The consensus has always been clear: Offense/Titans only.
"I’d never trust a guy who rocks some bling bling but can’t afford shoes."
- Valid life advice from the forums.

Base Karnov is like a rotary phone: it does one thing well, but you wouldn't use it to browse the internet. He serves a vital function in the Titan ecosystem, but in the fast-paced world of raids and wars, he is hopelessly outclassed. Keep him for the big yellow monsters, but leave him on the bench when fighting actual players.
